title: Building and Funding the Next Generation of Companies
subtitle: Billion Dollar Lessons: From Code to Cosmos
content: What happens when two of Portugal’s most visionary founders sit down to talk about building global companies from the edge of Europe? From first customers and team culture to international expansion and navigating the unicorn journey, they’ll share lessons learned, candid reflections.

title: Building and Funding the Next Generation of Companies
subtitle: Lessons from the Series A–B Transition
content: This panel brings together a diverse group of founders and investors to discuss the challenges and opportunities that come with raising a Series B round. Topics include evolving KPIs, building leadership teams, international expansion, and aligning with the right investors for long-term growth. Expect a lively discussion with multiple perspectives on what makes a company Series B–ready and the common pitfalls to avoid along the way.
speakers: Hugo Augusto / Inês Odila / Jaime Jorge / Verónica Orvalho

title: Building and Funding the Next Generation of Companies
subtitle: Funding the Unknown: How Venture Capital Is Adapting to Permanent Uncertainty
content: Venture capital was built to fund the future - but what happens when the future itself becomes harder to predict? As markets reset and volatility persists, investors are shifting from momentum-driven bets to resilience-driven strategies. How conviction is formed today? What makes companies fundable in uncertain times, and how venture itself is evolving to operate on the frontier?
speakers: Anton Turbakov / Sean Seton-Rodgers / Ricardo Jacinto / Pedro Carlos

title: Building and Funding the Next Generation of Companies
subtitle: Private Equity Partnerships: From Venture to Growth
content: Explain what later-stage investors look for in companies and how early-stage founders can prepare their business for growth rounds or eventual PE partnerships.
